diff --git a/breakout/fs/tests/test_run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py b/breakout/fs/tests/test_run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..8c9d21e7c72639e7d69c7bfcb1438bed6df114b3
--- /dev/null
+++ b/breakout/fs/tests/test_run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py
@@ -0,0 +1,93 @@
+import json
+import shutil
+import sys
+import time
+import unittest
+from pathlib import Path
+from unittest.mock import patch
+
+
+FS_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
+if str(FS_DIR) not in sys.path:
+    sys.path.insert(0, str(FS_DIR))
+
+import run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow as workflow  # noqa: E402
+
+
+class RunTwitterTop5MultiProductWorkflowTests(unittest.TestCase):
+    def make_case_root(self, case_name: str) -> Path:
+        root = FS_DIR / "tests" / "_tmp" / case_name
+        shutil.rmtree(root, ignore_errors=True)
+        root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+        return root
+
+    def test_validate_payload_accepts_compact_thread_posts(self) -> None:
+        root = self.make_case_root("top5_validate_accepts")
+        package_root = root / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package" / "2026-04-04"
+        package_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+
+        package_payload = {
+            "packages": [
+                {"product_type": "forex"},
+                {"product_type": "indices"},
+                {"product_type": "metals"},
+                {"product_type": "energy"},
+            ],
+            "top5_multi_product_thread_posts": [
+                "The Strategy Warehouse multi-product top 5 | Update at 2026-04-04 01:00 | Single-contract basis. Product detail posts follow. #StrategyWarehouse #FuturesTrading #AlgoTrading",
+                "Forex | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. GBPNZD brk R 2 tp50 sl5 1370 | Single-contract basis. #Forex #SystemTrading",
+                "Indices | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. NQ brk 2 tp20 sl5 7505 | Single-contract basis. #Indices #SystemTrading",
+                "Metals | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. SI brk R 2 tp30 sl5 11360 | Single-contract basis. #Metals #SystemTrading",
+                "Energy | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. CL brk 2 tp20 sl5 6630 | Single-contract basis. #Energy #SystemTrading",
+            ],
+        }
+        package_path = package_root / "top5_weekly_posting_package.json"
+        package_path.write_text(json.dumps(package_payload), encoding="utf-8")
+        now = time.time()
+        Path(package_path).touch()
+
+        with patch.object(workflow, "PACKAGE_ROOT", root / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package"):
+            ok, details, posts = workflow._validate_payload("2026-04-04", now)
+
+        self.assertTrue(ok)
+        self.assertIn("Validated 5 thread posts", details)
+        self.assertEqual(len(posts), 5)
+
+    def test_validate_payload_rejects_multiline_product_post(self) -> None:
+        root = self.make_case_root("top5_validate_rejects_multiline")
+        package_root = root / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package" / "2026-04-04"
+        package_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+
+        package_payload = {
+            "packages": [
+                {"product_type": "forex"},
+                {"product_type": "indices"},
+                {"product_type": "metals"},
+                {"product_type": "energy"},
+            ],
+            "top5_multi_product_thread_posts": [
+                "Lead post",
+                "Forex | Mar 30-Apr 05 |\n1. GBPNZD 1370 | Single-contract basis. #Forex #SystemTrading",
+                "Indices | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. NQ 7505 | Single-contract basis. #Indices #SystemTrading",
+                "Metals | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. SI 11360 | Single-contract basis. #Metals #SystemTrading",
+                "Energy | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. CL 6630 | Single-contract basis. #Energy #SystemTrading",
+            ],
+        }
+        package_path = package_root / "top5_weekly_posting_package.json"
+        package_path.write_text(json.dumps(package_payload), encoding="utf-8")
+        Path(package_path).touch()
+
+        with patch.object(workflow, "PACKAGE_ROOT", root / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package"):
+            ok, details, posts = workflow._validate_payload("2026-04-04", time.time())
+
+        self.assertFalse(ok)
+        self.assertIn("single-line compact entry", details)
+        self.assertEqual(posts, [])
+
+    @classmethod
+    def tearDownClass(cls) -> None:
+        shutil.rmtree(FS_DIR / "tests" / "_tmp", ignore_errors=True)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()
diff --git a/breakout/fs/tests/test_social_publisher_thread.py b/breakout/fs/tests/test_social_publisher_thread.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..fc6a1df043dfb9ee0670a391f901f0ce6f9c7a0d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/breakout/fs/tests/test_social_publisher_thread.py
@@ -0,0 +1,70 @@
+import sys
+import unittest
+from pathlib import Path
+from unittest.mock import Mock
+
+
+FS_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
+if str(FS_DIR) not in sys.path:
+    sys.path.insert(0, str(FS_DIR))
+
+from social_publisher import SocialPublisher  # noqa: E402
+
+
+class SocialPublisherThreadTests(unittest.TestCase):
+    def make_publisher(self) -> SocialPublisher:
+        publisher = SocialPublisher.__new__(SocialPublisher)
+        publisher.POST_CHAR_LIMIT = 280
+        publisher.api_enabled = True
+        publisher.post_log = {"posts": [], "last_post_time": None}
+        publisher._save_post_log = Mock()
+        publisher.can_post = Mock(return_value=(True, "ok"))
+        publisher._send_tweet_with_retries = Mock(
+            side_effect=[
+                {"success": True, "tweet_id": "111"},
+                {"success": True, "tweet_id": "222"},
+                {"success": True, "tweet_id": "333"},
+            ]
+        )
+        return publisher
+
+    def test_publish_thread_posts_replies_in_sequence(self) -> None:
+        publisher = self.make_publisher()
+
+        result = publisher.publish_thread(
+            ["Lead post", "Reply one", "Reply two"],
+            trigger="breakout_top5_multi_product_every_6_hours",
+        )
+
+        self.assertTrue(result["success"])
+        self.assertEqual(result["tweet_ids"], ["111", "222", "333"])
+        self.assertEqual(
+            publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.call_args_list[0].args,
+            ("Lead post",),
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(
+            publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.call_args_list[0].kwargs,
+            {"in_reply_to_tweet_id": None},
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(
+            publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.call_args_list[1].kwargs,
+            {"in_reply_to_tweet_id": "111"},
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(
+            publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.call_args_list[2].kwargs,
+            {"in_reply_to_tweet_id": "222"},
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(publisher.post_log["posts"][0]["thread_count"], 3)
+
+    def test_publish_thread_rejects_long_post(self) -> None:
+        publisher = self.make_publisher()
+
+        result = publisher.publish_thread(["x" * 281], trigger="test")
+
+        self.assertFalse(result["success"])
+        self.assertIn("exceeds 280", result["error"])
+        publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.assert_not_called()
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()

+def build_top5_thread_posts(
+    package_entries: List[Dict[str, Any]],
+    brand: str,
+    generated_at: str,
+) -> List[str]:
+    update_label = datetime.fromisoformat(generated_at).strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")

+        f"{brand} multi-product top 5 | Update at {update_label} | "
+        f"Single-contract basis. Product detail posts follow. {' '.join(CONSOLIDATED_HASHTAGS)}"

+
+    for entry in package_entries:

+        date_label = format_date_range(entry.get("date_range", []))
+        top_strategies = entry.get("top_strategies", [])

+            for item in top_strategies
+        )
+        hashtags = f"#{product_type.replace(' ', '')} #SystemTrading"
+        post_text = (
+            f"{product_type} | {date_label} | {ranking_text} | "
+            f"Single-contract basis. {hashtags}"
+        )
+        posts.append(post_text)
+
+    return posts
+
+
 def build_package(product_types: List[str], top_n: int, brand: str, live_date: Optional[str] = None, generated_at: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:

diff --git a/breakout/fs/run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py b/breakout/fs/run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..71379cbccb446f43c9ad788c8db4c4d5cce6eaf4
--- /dev/null
+++ b/breakout/fs/run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py
@@ -0,0 +1,177 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import datetime
+import json
+import subprocess
+import sys
+import time
+from pathlib import Path
+
+from social_publisher import SocialPublisher
+
+
+ROOT = Path(r"C:\Users\edebe\eds\TradeApps\breakout\fs")
+GENERATE_SCRIPT = ROOT / "tools" / "social_posting_package" / "generate_posting_package.py"
+WORKFLOW_STATUS = ROOT / "twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow_status.json"
+WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT = ROOT / "twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow_result.json"
+PACKAGE_ROOT = ROOT / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package"
+PACKAGE_FILENAME = "top5_weekly_posting_package.json"
+PACKAGE_PAYLOAD_KEY = "top5_multi_product_thread_posts"
+WORKFLOW_TRIGGER = "breakout_top5_multi_product_every_6_hours"
+
+
+def _write_status(status: dict) -> None:
+    WORKFLOW_STATUS.write_text(json.dumps(status, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
+
+
+def _new_status(run_date: str) -> dict:
+    return {
+        "timestamp": datetime.datetime.now().isoformat(),
+        "run_date": run_date,
+        "steps": {
+            "generate_content": {"ok": False, "details": "", "artifact": str(PACKAGE_ROOT / run_date / PACKAGE_FILENAME)},
+            "prepare_payload": {"ok": False, "details": "", "artifact": str(PACKAGE_ROOT / run_date / PACKAGE_FILENAME)},
+            "submit_post": {"ok": False, "details": "", "artifact": str(WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT)},
+            "record_outcome": {"ok": False, "details": "", "artifact": str(WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT)},
+        },
+        "final_status": "failed",
+    }
+
+
+def _mark_step(status: dict, step: str, ok: bool, details: str) -> None:
+    status["steps"][step]["ok"] = ok
+    status["steps"][step]["details"] = details
+    _write_status(status)
+
+
+def _run_command(command: list[str]) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
+    return subprocess.run(
+        command,
+        cwd=ROOT,
+        text=True,
+        encoding="utf-8",
+        errors="replace",
+        capture_output=True,
+        timeout=180000,
+    )
+
+
+def _load_package_json(run_date: str) -> dict:
+    package_path = PACKAGE_ROOT / run_date / PACKAGE_FILENAME
+    if not package_path.exists():
+        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Posting package not found: {package_path}")
+    return json.loads(package_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
+
+
+def _validate_payload(run_date: str, generated_started_at: float) -> tuple[bool, str, list[str]]:
+    package_path = PACKAGE_ROOT / run_date / PACKAGE_FILENAME
+    if not package_path.exists():
+        return False, f"Posting package was not created: {package_path}", []
+
+    if package_path.stat().st_mtime < generated_started_at - 1:
+        return False, f"Posting package was not refreshed by the current generator run: {package_path}", []
+
+    package = _load_package_json(run_date)
+    posts = package.get(PACKAGE_PAYLOAD_KEY)
+    if not isinstance(posts, list) or not posts:
+        return False, f"Posting package is missing {PACKAGE_PAYLOAD_KEY}", []
+
+    expected_count = 1 + len(package.get("packages", []))
+    if len(posts) != expected_count:
+        return False, f"Expected {expected_count} thread posts but found {len(posts)}", []
+
+    required_product_types = {"forex", "indices", "metals", "energy"}
+    seen_product_types: set[str] = set()
+    for index, post_text in enumerate(posts, start=1):
+        text = str(post_text or "").strip()
+        if not text:
+            return False, f"Thread post {index} is empty", []
+        if len(text) > 280:
+            return False, f"Thread post {index} exceeds 280 characters ({len(text)})", []
+        if index > 1:
+            if "\n" in text:
+                return False, f"Thread post {index} must remain a single-line compact entry", []
+            prefix = text.split("|", 1)[0].strip().lower()
+            seen_product_types.add(prefix)
+
+    missing = required_product_types - seen_product_types
+    if missing:
+        return False, f"Thread payload is missing product entries for: {sorted(missing)}", []
+
+    details = f"Validated {len(posts)} thread posts in {package_path}"
+    return True, details, [str(post).strip() for post in posts]
+
+
+def _write_result_artifact(run_date: str, thread_posts: list[str], publish_result: dict) -> None:
+    artifact = {
+        "timestamp": datetime.datetime.now().isoformat(),
+        "run_date": run_date,
+        "trigger": WORKFLOW_TRIGGER,
+        "thread_posts": [
+            {
+                "index": index,
+                "length": len(post_text),
+                "text": post_text,
+            }
+            for index, post_text in enumerate(thread_posts, start=1)
+        ],
+        "publish_result": publish_result,
+    }
+    WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT.write_text(json.dumps(artifact, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
+
+
+def main() -> int:
+    run_date = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else datetime.date.today().isoformat()
+    status = _new_status(run_date)
+    _write_status(status)
+
+    generate_started_at = time.time()
+    generate_result = _run_command(["python", str(GENERATE_SCRIPT), "--date", run_date])
+    if generate_result.returncode != 0:
+        _mark_step(
+            status,
+            "generate_content",
+            False,
+            f"Generator failed with code {generate_result.returncode}: {generate_result.stdout}\n{generate_result.stderr}",
+        )
+        return 1
+    _mark_step(status, "generate_content", True, generate_result.stdout.strip() or "Generator completed successfully")
+
+    try:
+        payload_ok, payload_details, thread_posts = _validate_payload(run_date, generate_started_at)
+    except Exception as exc:
+        _mark_step(status, "prepare_payload", False, f"Payload validation failed: {exc}")
+        return 1
+    if not payload_ok:
+        _mark_step(status, "prepare_payload", False, payload_details)
+        return 1
+    _mark_step(status, "prepare_payload", True, payload_details)
+
+    publisher = SocialPublisher()
+    publish_result = publisher.publish_thread(thread_posts, trigger=WORKFLOW_TRIGGER)
+    _write_result_artifact(run_date, thread_posts, publish_result)
+
+    if not publish_result.get("success"):
+        _mark_step(status, "submit_post", False, str(publish_result))
+        return 1
+    _mark_step(status, "submit_post", True, str(publish_result))
+
+    tweet_ids = publish_result.get("tweet_ids") or []
+    thread_urls = publish_result.get("thread_urls") or []
+    if not tweet_ids:
+        _mark_step(status, "record_outcome", False, f"No tweet IDs returned: {publish_result}")
+        return 1
+
+    _mark_step(
+        status,
+        "record_outcome",
+        True,
+        f"Recorded {len(tweet_ids)} tweet IDs and {len(thread_urls)} URLs in {WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT}",
+    )
+    status["final_status"] = "success"
+    _write_status(status)
+    return 0
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    raise SystemExit(main())

+        return result
+
+    def publish_thread(self, posts: List[str], trigger: str = "manual_thread") -> Dict:
+        """
+        Publish a reply thread where each post replies to the previous post.
+
+        Args:
+            posts: Ordered list of prepared post bodies.
+            trigger: Trigger name for rate-limit and audit logging.
+
+        Returns:
+            Result dict with thread publish details.
+        """
+        normalized_posts = [str(post or "").strip() for post in posts]
+        if not normalized_posts or not any(normalized_posts):
+            return {"success": False, "error": "At least one non-empty post is required"}
+
+        for index, post_text in enumerate(normalized_posts, start=1):
+            if not post_text:
+                return {"success": False, "error": f"Thread item {index} is empty"}
+            if len(post_text) > self.POST_CHAR_LIMIT:
+                return {
+                    "success": False,
+                    "error": f"Thread item {index} exceeds {self.POST_CHAR_LIMIT} character limit",
+                    "length": len(post_text),
+                    "index": index,
+                }
+
+        can_post, reason = self.can_post(trigger=trigger)
+        if not can_post:
+            return {"success": False, "error": reason}
+
+        results: List[Dict] = []
+        previous_tweet_id: Optional[str] = None
+
+        if not self.api_enabled:
+            for index, post_text in enumerate(normalized_posts, start=1):
+                results.append(
+                    {
+                        "success": True,
+                        "dry_run": True,
+                        "message": "API not configured - dry run mode",
+                        "tweet_id": f"dry_run_{index}",
+                        "text": post_text,
+                        "reply_to_tweet_id": previous_tweet_id,
+                    }
+                )
+                previous_tweet_id = f"dry_run_{index}"
+        else:
+            for post_text in normalized_posts:
+                try:
+                    result = self._send_tweet_with_retries(post_text, in_reply_to_tweet_id=previous_tweet_id)
+                except Exception as exc:
+                    return {"success": False, "error": str(exc), "results": results}
+                if not result.get("success"):
+                    result["results"] = results
+                    return result
+                results.append(result)
+                previous_tweet_id = result.get("tweet_id")
+
+        tweet_ids = [str(result.get("tweet_id")) for result in results if result.get("tweet_id")]
+        thread_urls = [f"https://x.com/i/web/status/{tweet_id}" for tweet_id in tweet_ids]
+
+        post_entry = {
+            "date": datetime.now().isoformat(),
+            "trigger": trigger,
+            "text": normalized_posts[0][:100] + "..." if len(normalized_posts[0]) > 100 else normalized_posts[0],
+            "success": True,
+            "dry_run": all(result.get("dry_run", False) for result in results),
+            "thread": True,
+            "thread_count": len(normalized_posts),
+            "tweet_ids": tweet_ids,
+        }
+        self.post_log["posts"].insert(0, post_entry)
+        self.post_log["posts"] = self.post_log["posts"][:100]
+        self.post_log["last_post_time"] = datetime.now().isoformat()
+        self._save_post_log()
+
+        return {
+            "success": True,
+            "message": "Thread posted successfully",
+            "tweet_ids": tweet_ids,
+            "thread_urls": thread_urls,
+            "results": results,
+        }
+
+    def _send_tweet(self, text: str, in_reply_to_tweet_id: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict:
+        """
+        Send a tweet using Twitter API v2.
+
+        Requires tweepy library: pip install tweepy
+        """
         try:
             import tweepy
         except ImportError:
@@ -450,16 +534,21 @@
                 access_token_secret=self._get_twitter_credential("twitter_access_secret", "TWITTER_ACCESS_SECRET")
             )

-                "message": "Tweet posted successfully"

-

diff --git a/breakout/fs/tests/test_run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py b/breakout/fs/tests/test_run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..8c9d21e7c72639e7d69c7bfcb1438bed6df114b3
--- /dev/null
+++ b/breakout/fs/tests/test_run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py
@@ -0,0 +1,93 @@
+import json
+import shutil
+import sys
+import time
+import unittest
+from pathlib import Path
+from unittest.mock import patch
+
+
+FS_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
+if str(FS_DIR) not in sys.path:
+    sys.path.insert(0, str(FS_DIR))
+
+import run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow as workflow  # noqa: E402
+
+
+class RunTwitterTop5MultiProductWorkflowTests(unittest.TestCase):
+    def make_case_root(self, case_name: str) -> Path:
+        root = FS_DIR / "tests" / "_tmp" / case_name
+        shutil.rmtree(root, ignore_errors=True)
+        root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+        return root
+
+    def test_validate_payload_accepts_compact_thread_posts(self) -> None:
+        root = self.make_case_root("top5_validate_accepts")
+        package_root = root / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package" / "2026-04-04"
+        package_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+
+        package_payload = {
+            "packages": [
+                {"product_type": "forex"},
+                {"product_type": "indices"},
+                {"product_type": "metals"},
+                {"product_type": "energy"},
+            ],
+            "top5_multi_product_thread_posts": [
+                "The Strategy Warehouse multi-product top 5 | Update at 2026-04-04 01:00 | Single-contract basis. Product detail posts follow. #StrategyWarehouse #FuturesTrading #AlgoTrading",
+                "Forex | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. GBPNZD brk R 2 tp50 sl5 1370 | Single-contract basis. #Forex #SystemTrading",
+                "Indices | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. NQ brk 2 tp20 sl5 7505 | Single-contract basis. #Indices #SystemTrading",
+                "Metals | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. SI brk R 2 tp30 sl5 11360 | Single-contract basis. #Metals #SystemTrading",
+                "Energy | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. CL brk 2 tp20 sl5 6630 | Single-contract basis. #Energy #SystemTrading",
+            ],
+        }
+        package_path = package_root / "top5_weekly_posting_package.json"
+        package_path.write_text(json.dumps(package_payload), encoding="utf-8")
+        now = time.time()
+        Path(package_path).touch()
+
+        with patch.object(workflow, "PACKAGE_ROOT", root / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package"):
+            ok, details, posts = workflow._validate_payload("2026-04-04", now)
+
+        self.assertTrue(ok)
+        self.assertIn("Validated 5 thread posts", details)
+        self.assertEqual(len(posts), 5)
+
+    def test_validate_payload_rejects_multiline_product_post(self) -> None:
+        root = self.make_case_root("top5_validate_rejects_multiline")
+        package_root = root / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package" / "2026-04-04"
+        package_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+
+        package_payload = {
+            "packages": [
+                {"product_type": "forex"},
+                {"product_type": "indices"},
+                {"product_type": "metals"},
+                {"product_type": "energy"},
+            ],
+            "top5_multi_product_thread_posts": [
+                "Lead post",
+                "Forex | Mar 30-Apr 05 |\n1. GBPNZD 1370 | Single-contract basis. #Forex #SystemTrading",
+                "Indices | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. NQ 7505 | Single-contract basis. #Indices #SystemTrading",
+                "Metals | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. SI 11360 | Single-contract basis. #Metals #SystemTrading",
+                "Energy | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. CL 6630 | Single-contract basis. #Energy #SystemTrading",
+            ],
+        }
+        package_path = package_root / "top5_weekly_posting_package.json"
+        package_path.write_text(json.dumps(package_payload), encoding="utf-8")
+        Path(package_path).touch()
+
+        with patch.object(workflow, "PACKAGE_ROOT", root / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package"):
+            ok, details, posts = workflow._validate_payload("2026-04-04", time.time())
+
+        self.assertFalse(ok)
+        self.assertIn("single-line compact entry", details)
+        self.assertEqual(posts, [])
+
+    @classmethod
+    def tearDownClass(cls) -> None:
+        shutil.rmtree(FS_DIR / "tests" / "_tmp", ignore_errors=True)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()
diff --git a/breakout/fs/tests/test_social_publisher_thread.py b/breakout/fs/tests/test_social_publisher_thread.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..fc6a1df043dfb9ee0670a391f901f0ce6f9c7a0d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/breakout/fs/tests/test_social_publisher_thread.py
@@ -0,0 +1,70 @@
+import sys
+import unittest
+from pathlib import Path
+from unittest.mock import Mock
+
+
+FS_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
+if str(FS_DIR) not in sys.path:
+    sys.path.insert(0, str(FS_DIR))
+
+from social_publisher import SocialPublisher  # noqa: E402
+
+
+class SocialPublisherThreadTests(unittest.TestCase):
+    def make_publisher(self) -> SocialPublisher:
+        publisher = SocialPublisher.__new__(SocialPublisher)
+        publisher.POST_CHAR_LIMIT = 280
+        publisher.api_enabled = True
+        publisher.post_log = {"posts": [], "last_post_time": None}
+        publisher._save_post_log = Mock()
+        publisher.can_post = Mock(return_value=(True, "ok"))
+        publisher._send_tweet_with_retries = Mock(
+            side_effect=[
+                {"success": True, "tweet_id": "111"},
+                {"success": True, "tweet_id": "222"},
+                {"success": True, "tweet_id": "333"},
+            ]
+        )
+        return publisher
+
+    def test_publish_thread_posts_replies_in_sequence(self) -> None:
+        publisher = self.make_publisher()
+
+        result = publisher.publish_thread(
+            ["Lead post", "Reply one", "Reply two"],
+            trigger="breakout_top5_multi_product_every_6_hours",
+        )
+
+        self.assertTrue(result["success"])
+        self.assertEqual(result["tweet_ids"], ["111", "222", "333"])
+        self.assertEqual(
+            publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.call_args_list[0].args,
+            ("Lead post",),
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(
+            publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.call_args_list[0].kwargs,
+            {"in_reply_to_tweet_id": None},
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(
+            publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.call_args_list[1].kwargs,
+            {"in_reply_to_tweet_id": "111"},
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(
+            publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.call_args_list[2].kwargs,
+            {"in_reply_to_tweet_id": "222"},
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(publisher.post_log["posts"][0]["thread_count"], 3)
+
+    def test_publish_thread_rejects_long_post(self) -> None:
+        publisher = self.make_publisher()
+
+        result = publisher.publish_thread(["x" * 281], trigger="test")
+
+        self.assertFalse(result["success"])
+        self.assertIn("exceeds 280", result["error"])
+        publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.assert_not_called()
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()

+++ b/breakout/fs/tools/social_posting_package/generate_posting_package.py
@@ -21,6 +21,7 @@
 JSON_ROOT = FS_ROOT / "json" / "live"
 TEMP_TWEET_PATH = FS_ROOT / "temp_tweet.txt"
 TOP2_TEMP_TWEET_PATH = FS_ROOT / "temp_tweet_top2.txt"

+            f"Single-contract basis. {hashtags}"
+        )
+        posts.append(post_text)
+
+    return posts
+

diff --git a/breakout/fs/run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py b/breakout/fs/run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..71379cbccb446f43c9ad788c8db4c4d5cce6eaf4
--- /dev/null
+++ b/breakout/fs/run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py
@@ -0,0 +1,177 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import datetime
+import json
+import subprocess
+import sys
+import time
+from pathlib import Path
+
+from social_publisher import SocialPublisher
+
+
+ROOT = Path(r"C:\Users\edebe\eds\TradeApps\breakout\fs")
+GENERATE_SCRIPT = ROOT / "tools" / "social_posting_package" / "generate_posting_package.py"
+WORKFLOW_STATUS = ROOT / "twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow_status.json"
+WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT = ROOT / "twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow_result.json"
+PACKAGE_ROOT = ROOT / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package"
+PACKAGE_FILENAME = "top5_weekly_posting_package.json"
+PACKAGE_PAYLOAD_KEY = "top5_multi_product_thread_posts"
+WORKFLOW_TRIGGER = "breakout_top5_multi_product_every_6_hours"
+
+
+def _write_status(status: dict) -> None:
+    WORKFLOW_STATUS.write_text(json.dumps(status, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
+
+
+def _new_status(run_date: str) -> dict:
+    return {
+        "timestamp": datetime.datetime.now().isoformat(),
+        "run_date": run_date,
+        "steps": {
+            "generate_content": {"ok": False, "details": "", "artifact": str(PACKAGE_ROOT / run_date / PACKAGE_FILENAME)},
+            "prepare_payload": {"ok": False, "details": "", "artifact": str(PACKAGE_ROOT / run_date / PACKAGE_FILENAME)},
+            "submit_post": {"ok": False, "details": "", "artifact": str(WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT)},
+            "record_outcome": {"ok": False, "details": "", "artifact": str(WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT)},
+        },
+        "final_status": "failed",
+    }
+
+
+def _mark_step(status: dict, step: str, ok: bool, details: str) -> None:
+    status["steps"][step]["ok"] = ok
+    status["steps"][step]["details"] = details
+    _write_status(status)
+
+
+def _run_command(command: list[str]) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
+    return subprocess.run(
+        command,
+        cwd=ROOT,
+        text=True,
+        encoding="utf-8",
+        errors="replace",
+        capture_output=True,
+        timeout=180000,
+    )
+
+
+def _load_package_json(run_date: str) -> dict:
+    package_path = PACKAGE_ROOT / run_date / PACKAGE_FILENAME
+    if not package_path.exists():
+        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Posting package not found: {package_path}")
+    return json.loads(package_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
+
+
+def _validate_payload(run_date: str, generated_started_at: float) -> tuple[bool, str, list[str]]:
+    package_path = PACKAGE_ROOT / run_date / PACKAGE_FILENAME
+    if not package_path.exists():
+        return False, f"Posting package was not created: {package_path}", []
+
+    if package_path.stat().st_mtime < generated_started_at - 1:
+        return False, f"Posting package was not refreshed by the current generator run: {package_path}", []
+
+    package = _load_package_json(run_date)
+    posts = package.get(PACKAGE_PAYLOAD_KEY)
+    if not isinstance(posts, list) or not posts:
+        return False, f"Posting package is missing {PACKAGE_PAYLOAD_KEY}", []
+
+    expected_count = 1 + len(package.get("packages", []))
+    if len(posts) != expected_count:
+        return False, f"Expected {expected_count} thread posts but found {len(posts)}", []
+
+    required_product_types = {"forex", "indices", "metals", "energy"}
+    seen_product_types: set[str] = set()
+    for index, post_text in enumerate(posts, start=1):
+        text = str(post_text or "").strip()
+        if not text:
+            return False, f"Thread post {index} is empty", []
+        if len(text) > 280:
+            return False, f"Thread post {index} exceeds 280 characters ({len(text)})", []
+        if index > 1:
+            if "\n" in text:
+                return False, f"Thread post {index} must remain a single-line compact entry", []
+            prefix = text.split("|", 1)[0].strip().lower()
+            seen_product_types.add(prefix)
+
+    missing = required_product_types - seen_product_types
+    if missing:
+        return False, f"Thread payload is missing product entries for: {sorted(missing)}", []
+
+    details = f"Validated {len(posts)} thread posts in {package_path}"
+    return True, details, [str(post).strip() for post in posts]
+
+
+def _write_result_artifact(run_date: str, thread_posts: list[str], publish_result: dict) -> None:
+    artifact = {
+        "timestamp": datetime.datetime.now().isoformat(),
+        "run_date": run_date,
+        "trigger": WORKFLOW_TRIGGER,
+        "thread_posts": [
+            {
+                "index": index,
+                "length": len(post_text),
+                "text": post_text,
+            }
+            for index, post_text in enumerate(thread_posts, start=1)
+        ],
+        "publish_result": publish_result,
+    }
+    WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT.write_text(json.dumps(artifact, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
+
+
+def main() -> int:
+    run_date = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else datetime.date.today().isoformat()
+    status = _new_status(run_date)
+    _write_status(status)
+
+    generate_started_at = time.time()
+    generate_result = _run_command(["python", str(GENERATE_SCRIPT), "--date", run_date])
+    if generate_result.returncode != 0:
+        _mark_step(
+            status,
+            "generate_content",
+            False,
+            f"Generator failed with code {generate_result.returncode}: {generate_result.stdout}\n{generate_result.stderr}",
+        )
+        return 1
+    _mark_step(status, "generate_content", True, generate_result.stdout.strip() or "Generator completed successfully")
+
+    try:
+        payload_ok, payload_details, thread_posts = _validate_payload(run_date, generate_started_at)
+    except Exception as exc:
+        _mark_step(status, "prepare_payload", False, f"Payload validation failed: {exc}")
+        return 1
+    if not payload_ok:
+        _mark_step(status, "prepare_payload", False, payload_details)
+        return 1
+    _mark_step(status, "prepare_payload", True, payload_details)
+
+    publisher = SocialPublisher()
+    publish_result = publisher.publish_thread(thread_posts, trigger=WORKFLOW_TRIGGER)
+    _write_result_artifact(run_date, thread_posts, publish_result)
+
+    if not publish_result.get("success"):
+        _mark_step(status, "submit_post", False, str(publish_result))
+        return 1
+    _mark_step(status, "submit_post", True, str(publish_result))
+
+    tweet_ids = publish_result.get("tweet_ids") or []
+    thread_urls = publish_result.get("thread_urls") or []
+    if not tweet_ids:
+        _mark_step(status, "record_outcome", False, f"No tweet IDs returned: {publish_result}")
+        return 1
+
+    _mark_step(
+        status,
+        "record_outcome",
+        True,
+        f"Recorded {len(tweet_ids)} tweet IDs and {len(thread_urls)} URLs in {WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT}",
+    )
+    status["final_status"] = "success"
+    _write_status(status)
+    return 0
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    raise SystemExit(main())

             if result.get("success"):

@@ -424,16 +424,100 @@
         self.post_log["posts"].insert(0, post_entry)
         self.post_log["posts"] = self.post_log["posts"][:100]  # Keep last 100

-        Send a tweet using Twitter API v2.
-
-        Requires tweepy library: pip install tweepy
-        """
+        self._save_post_log()
+
+        return result
+
+    def publish_thread(self, posts: List[str], trigger: str = "manual_thread") -> Dict:
+        """
+        Publish a reply thread where each post replies to the previous post.
+
+        Args:
+            posts: Ordered list of prepared post bodies.
+            trigger: Trigger name for rate-limit and audit logging.
+
+        Returns:
+            Result dict with thread publish details.
+        """
+        normalized_posts = [str(post or "").strip() for post in posts]
+        if not normalized_posts or not any(normalized_posts):
+            return {"success": False, "error": "At least one non-empty post is required"}
+
+        for index, post_text in enumerate(normalized_posts, start=1):
+            if not post_text:
+                return {"success": False, "error": f"Thread item {index} is empty"}
+            if len(post_text) > self.POST_CHAR_LIMIT:
+                return {
+                    "success": False,
+                    "error": f"Thread item {index} exceeds {self.POST_CHAR_LIMIT} character limit",
+                    "length": len(post_text),
+                    "index": index,
+                }
+
+        can_post, reason = self.can_post(trigger=trigger)
+        if not can_post:
+            return {"success": False, "error": reason}
+
+        results: List[Dict] = []
+        previous_tweet_id: Optional[str] = None
+
+        if not self.api_enabled:
+            for index, post_text in enumerate(normalized_posts, start=1):
+                results.append(
+                    {
+                        "success": True,
+                        "dry_run": True,
+                        "message": "API not configured - dry run mode",
+                        "tweet_id": f"dry_run_{index}",
+                        "text": post_text,
+                        "reply_to_tweet_id": previous_tweet_id,
+                    }
+                )
+                previous_tweet_id = f"dry_run_{index}"
+        else:
+            for post_text in normalized_posts:
+                try:
+                    result = self._send_tweet_with_retries(post_text, in_reply_to_tweet_id=previous_tweet_id)
+                except Exception as exc:
+                    return {"success": False, "error": str(exc), "results": results}
+                if not result.get("success"):
+                    result["results"] = results
+                    return result
+                results.append(result)
+                previous_tweet_id = result.get("tweet_id")
+
+        tweet_ids = [str(result.get("tweet_id")) for result in results if result.get("tweet_id")]
+        thread_urls = [f"https://x.com/i/web/status/{tweet_id}" for tweet_id in tweet_ids]
+
+        post_entry = {
+            "date": datetime.now().isoformat(),
+            "trigger": trigger,
+            "text": normalized_posts[0][:100] + "..." if len(normalized_posts[0]) > 100 else normalized_posts[0],
+            "success": True,
+            "dry_run": all(result.get("dry_run", False) for result in results),
+            "thread": True,
+            "thread_count": len(normalized_posts),
+            "tweet_ids": tweet_ids,
+        }
+        self.post_log["posts"].insert(0, post_entry)
+        self.post_log["posts"] = self.post_log["posts"][:100]
+        self.post_log["last_post_time"] = datetime.now().isoformat()
+        self._save_post_log()
+
+        return {
+            "success": True,
+            "message": "Thread posted successfully",
+            "tweet_ids": tweet_ids,
+            "thread_urls": thread_urls,
+            "results": results,
+        }
+
+    def _send_tweet(self, text: str, in_reply_to_tweet_id: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict:
+        """
+        Send a tweet using Twitter API v2.
+
+        Requires tweepy library: pip install tweepy
+        """
         try:
             import tweepy
         except ImportError:
@@ -450,16 +534,21 @@
                 access_token_secret=self._get_twitter_credential("twitter_access_secret", "TWITTER_ACCESS_SECRET")
             )

-
-            return {
-                "success": True,
-                "tweet_id": response.data["id"],
-                "message": "Tweet posted successfully"
-            }
-
-        except tweepy.TweepyException as e:
-            return {"success": False, "error": str(e)}

diff --git a/breakout/fs/tests/test_run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py b/breakout/fs/tests/test_run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..8c9d21e7c72639e7d69c7bfcb1438bed6df114b3
--- /dev/null
+++ b/breakout/fs/tests/test_run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py
@@ -0,0 +1,93 @@
+import json
+import shutil
+import sys
+import time
+import unittest
+from pathlib import Path
+from unittest.mock import patch
+
+
+FS_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
+if str(FS_DIR) not in sys.path:
+    sys.path.insert(0, str(FS_DIR))
+
+import run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow as workflow  # noqa: E402
+
+
+class RunTwitterTop5MultiProductWorkflowTests(unittest.TestCase):
+    def make_case_root(self, case_name: str) -> Path:
+        root = FS_DIR / "tests" / "_tmp" / case_name
+        shutil.rmtree(root, ignore_errors=True)
+        root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+        return root
+
+    def test_validate_payload_accepts_compact_thread_posts(self) -> None:
+        root = self.make_case_root("top5_validate_accepts")
+        package_root = root / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package" / "2026-04-04"
+        package_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+
+        package_payload = {
+            "packages": [
+                {"product_type": "forex"},
+                {"product_type": "indices"},
+                {"product_type": "metals"},
+                {"product_type": "energy"},
+            ],
+            "top5_multi_product_thread_posts": [
+                "The Strategy Warehouse multi-product top 5 | Update at 2026-04-04 01:00 | Single-contract basis. Product detail posts follow. #StrategyWarehouse #FuturesTrading #AlgoTrading",
+                "Forex | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. GBPNZD brk R 2 tp50 sl5 1370 | Single-contract basis. #Forex #SystemTrading",
+                "Indices | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. NQ brk 2 tp20 sl5 7505 | Single-contract basis. #Indices #SystemTrading",
+                "Metals | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. SI brk R 2 tp30 sl5 11360 | Single-contract basis. #Metals #SystemTrading",
+                "Energy | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. CL brk 2 tp20 sl5 6630 | Single-contract basis. #Energy #SystemTrading",
+            ],
+        }
+        package_path = package_root / "top5_weekly_posting_package.json"
+        package_path.write_text(json.dumps(package_payload), encoding="utf-8")
+        now = time.time()
+        Path(package_path).touch()
+
+        with patch.object(workflow, "PACKAGE_ROOT", root / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package"):
+            ok, details, posts = workflow._validate_payload("2026-04-04", now)
+
+        self.assertTrue(ok)
+        self.assertIn("Validated 5 thread posts", details)
+        self.assertEqual(len(posts), 5)
+
+    def test_validate_payload_rejects_multiline_product_post(self) -> None:
+        root = self.make_case_root("top5_validate_rejects_multiline")
+        package_root = root / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package" / "2026-04-04"
+        package_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+
+        package_payload = {
+            "packages": [
+                {"product_type": "forex"},
+                {"product_type": "indices"},
+                {"product_type": "metals"},
+                {"product_type": "energy"},
+            ],
+            "top5_multi_product_thread_posts": [
+                "Lead post",
+                "Forex | Mar 30-Apr 05 |\n1. GBPNZD 1370 | Single-contract basis. #Forex #SystemTrading",
+                "Indices | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. NQ 7505 | Single-contract basis. #Indices #SystemTrading",
+                "Metals | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. SI 11360 | Single-contract basis. #Metals #SystemTrading",
+                "Energy | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. CL 6630 | Single-contract basis. #Energy #SystemTrading",
+            ],
+        }
+        package_path = package_root / "top5_weekly_posting_package.json"
+        package_path.write_text(json.dumps(package_payload), encoding="utf-8")
+        Path(package_path).touch()
+
+        with patch.object(workflow, "PACKAGE_ROOT", root / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package"):
+            ok, details, posts = workflow._validate_payload("2026-04-04", time.time())
+
+        self.assertFalse(ok)
+        self.assertIn("single-line compact entry", details)
+        self.assertEqual(posts, [])
+
+    @classmethod
+    def tearDownClass(cls) -> None:
+        shutil.rmtree(FS_DIR / "tests" / "_tmp", ignore_errors=True)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()
diff --git a/breakout/fs/tests/test_social_publisher_thread.py b/breakout/fs/tests/test_social_publisher_thread.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..fc6a1df043dfb9ee0670a391f901f0ce6f9c7a0d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/breakout/fs/tests/test_social_publisher_thread.py
@@ -0,0 +1,70 @@
+import sys
+import unittest
+from pathlib import Path
+from unittest.mock import Mock
+
+
+FS_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
+if str(FS_DIR) not in sys.path:
+    sys.path.insert(0, str(FS_DIR))
+
+from social_publisher import SocialPublisher  # noqa: E402
+
+
+class SocialPublisherThreadTests(unittest.TestCase):
+    def make_publisher(self) -> SocialPublisher:
+        publisher = SocialPublisher.__new__(SocialPublisher)
+        publisher.POST_CHAR_LIMIT = 280
+        publisher.api_enabled = True
+        publisher.post_log = {"posts": [], "last_post_time": None}
+        publisher._save_post_log = Mock()
+        publisher.can_post = Mock(return_value=(True, "ok"))
+        publisher._send_tweet_with_retries = Mock(
+            side_effect=[
+                {"success": True, "tweet_id": "111"},
+                {"success": True, "tweet_id": "222"},
+                {"success": True, "tweet_id": "333"},
+            ]
+        )
+        return publisher
+
+    def test_publish_thread_posts_replies_in_sequence(self) -> None:
+        publisher = self.make_publisher()
+
+        result = publisher.publish_thread(
+            ["Lead post", "Reply one", "Reply two"],
+            trigger="breakout_top5_multi_product_every_6_hours",
+        )
+
+        self.assertTrue(result["success"])
+        self.assertEqual(result["tweet_ids"], ["111", "222", "333"])
+        self.assertEqual(
+            publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.call_args_list[0].args,
+            ("Lead post",),
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(
+            publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.call_args_list[0].kwargs,
+            {"in_reply_to_tweet_id": None},
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(
+            publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.call_args_list[1].kwargs,
+            {"in_reply_to_tweet_id": "111"},
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(
+            publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.call_args_list[2].kwargs,
+            {"in_reply_to_tweet_id": "222"},
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(publisher.post_log["posts"][0]["thread_count"], 3)
+
+    def test_publish_thread_rejects_long_post(self) -> None:
+        publisher = self.make_publisher()
+
+        result = publisher.publish_thread(["x" * 281], trigger="test")
+
+        self.assertFalse(result["success"])
+        self.assertIn("exceeds 280", result["error"])
+        publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.assert_not_called()
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()

+            f"Single-contract basis. {hashtags}"
+        )
+        posts.append(post_text)
+
+    return posts
+

diff --git a/breakout/fs/run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py b/breakout/fs/run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..71379cbccb446f43c9ad788c8db4c4d5cce6eaf4
--- /dev/null
+++ b/breakout/fs/run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py
@@ -0,0 +1,177 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import datetime
+import json
+import subprocess
+import sys
+import time
+from pathlib import Path
+
+from social_publisher import SocialPublisher
+
+
+ROOT = Path(r"C:\Users\edebe\eds\TradeApps\breakout\fs")
+GENERATE_SCRIPT = ROOT / "tools" / "social_posting_package" / "generate_posting_package.py"
+WORKFLOW_STATUS = ROOT / "twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow_status.json"
+WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT = ROOT / "twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow_result.json"
+PACKAGE_ROOT = ROOT / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package"
+PACKAGE_FILENAME = "top5_weekly_posting_package.json"
+PACKAGE_PAYLOAD_KEY = "top5_multi_product_thread_posts"
+WORKFLOW_TRIGGER = "breakout_top5_multi_product_every_6_hours"
+
+
+def _write_status(status: dict) -> None:
+    WORKFLOW_STATUS.write_text(json.dumps(status, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
+
+
+def _new_status(run_date: str) -> dict:
+    return {
+        "timestamp": datetime.datetime.now().isoformat(),
+        "run_date": run_date,
+        "steps": {
+            "generate_content": {"ok": False, "details": "", "artifact": str(PACKAGE_ROOT / run_date / PACKAGE_FILENAME)},
+            "prepare_payload": {"ok": False, "details": "", "artifact": str(PACKAGE_ROOT / run_date / PACKAGE_FILENAME)},
+            "submit_post": {"ok": False, "details": "", "artifact": str(WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT)},
+            "record_outcome": {"ok": False, "details": "", "artifact": str(WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT)},
+        },
+        "final_status": "failed",
+    }
+
+
+def _mark_step(status: dict, step: str, ok: bool, details: str) -> None:
+    status["steps"][step]["ok"] = ok
+    status["steps"][step]["details"] = details
+    _write_status(status)
+
+
+def _run_command(command: list[str]) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
+    return subprocess.run(
+        command,
+        cwd=ROOT,
+        text=True,
+        encoding="utf-8",
+        errors="replace",
+        capture_output=True,
+        timeout=180000,
+    )
+
+
+def _load_package_json(run_date: str) -> dict:
+    package_path = PACKAGE_ROOT / run_date / PACKAGE_FILENAME
+    if not package_path.exists():
+        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Posting package not found: {package_path}")
+    return json.loads(package_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
+
+
+def _validate_payload(run_date: str, generated_started_at: float) -> tuple[bool, str, list[str]]:
+    package_path = PACKAGE_ROOT / run_date / PACKAGE_FILENAME
+    if not package_path.exists():
+        return False, f"Posting package was not created: {package_path}", []
+
+    if package_path.stat().st_mtime < generated_started_at - 1:
+        return False, f"Posting package was not refreshed by the current generator run: {package_path}", []
+
+    package = _load_package_json(run_date)
+    posts = package.get(PACKAGE_PAYLOAD_KEY)
+    if not isinstance(posts, list) or not posts:
+        return False, f"Posting package is missing {PACKAGE_PAYLOAD_KEY}", []
+
+    expected_count = 1 + len(package.get("packages", []))
+    if len(posts) != expected_count:
+        return False, f"Expected {expected_count} thread posts but found {len(posts)}", []
+
+    required_product_types = {"forex", "indices", "metals", "energy"}
+    seen_product_types: set[str] = set()
+    for index, post_text in enumerate(posts, start=1):
+        text = str(post_text or "").strip()
+        if not text:
+            return False, f"Thread post {index} is empty", []
+        if len(text) > 280:
+            return False, f"Thread post {index} exceeds 280 characters ({len(text)})", []
+        if index > 1:
+            if "\n" in text:
+                return False, f"Thread post {index} must remain a single-line compact entry", []
+            prefix = text.split("|", 1)[0].strip().lower()
+            seen_product_types.add(prefix)
+
+    missing = required_product_types - seen_product_types
+    if missing:
+        return False, f"Thread payload is missing product entries for: {sorted(missing)}", []
+
+    details = f"Validated {len(posts)} thread posts in {package_path}"
+    return True, details, [str(post).strip() for post in posts]
+
+
+def _write_result_artifact(run_date: str, thread_posts: list[str], publish_result: dict) -> None:
+    artifact = {
+        "timestamp": datetime.datetime.now().isoformat(),
+        "run_date": run_date,
+        "trigger": WORKFLOW_TRIGGER,
+        "thread_posts": [
+            {
+                "index": index,
+                "length": len(post_text),
+                "text": post_text,
+            }
+            for index, post_text in enumerate(thread_posts, start=1)
+        ],
+        "publish_result": publish_result,
+    }
+    WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT.write_text(json.dumps(artifact, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
+
+
+def main() -> int:
+    run_date = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else datetime.date.today().isoformat()
+    status = _new_status(run_date)
+    _write_status(status)
+
+    generate_started_at = time.time()
+    generate_result = _run_command(["python", str(GENERATE_SCRIPT), "--date", run_date])
+    if generate_result.returncode != 0:
+        _mark_step(
+            status,
+            "generate_content",
+            False,
+            f"Generator failed with code {generate_result.returncode}: {generate_result.stdout}\n{generate_result.stderr}",
+        )
+        return 1
+    _mark_step(status, "generate_content", True, generate_result.stdout.strip() or "Generator completed successfully")
+
+    try:
+        payload_ok, payload_details, thread_posts = _validate_payload(run_date, generate_started_at)
+    except Exception as exc:
+        _mark_step(status, "prepare_payload", False, f"Payload validation failed: {exc}")
+        return 1
+    if not payload_ok:
+        _mark_step(status, "prepare_payload", False, payload_details)
+        return 1
+    _mark_step(status, "prepare_payload", True, payload_details)
+
+    publisher = SocialPublisher()
+    publish_result = publisher.publish_thread(thread_posts, trigger=WORKFLOW_TRIGGER)
+    _write_result_artifact(run_date, thread_posts, publish_result)
+
+    if not publish_result.get("success"):
+        _mark_step(status, "submit_post", False, str(publish_result))
+        return 1
+    _mark_step(status, "submit_post", True, str(publish_result))
+
+    tweet_ids = publish_result.get("tweet_ids") or []
+    thread_urls = publish_result.get("thread_urls") or []
+    if not tweet_ids:
+        _mark_step(status, "record_outcome", False, f"No tweet IDs returned: {publish_result}")
+        return 1
+
+    _mark_step(
+        status,
+        "record_outcome",
+        True,
+        f"Recorded {len(tweet_ids)} tweet IDs and {len(thread_urls)} URLs in {WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT}",
+    )
+    status["final_status"] = "success"
+    _write_status(status)
+    return 0
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    raise SystemExit(main())

@@ -424,16 +424,100 @@
         self.post_log["posts"].insert(0, post_entry)

+
+        return result
+
+    def publish_thread(self, posts: List[str], trigger: str = "manual_thread") -> Dict:
+        """
+        Publish a reply thread where each post replies to the previous post.
+
+        Args:
+            posts: Ordered list of prepared post bodies.
+            trigger: Trigger name for rate-limit and audit logging.
+
+        Returns:
+            Result dict with thread publish details.
+        """
+        normalized_posts = [str(post or "").strip() for post in posts]
+        if not normalized_posts or not any(normalized_posts):
+            return {"success": False, "error": "At least one non-empty post is required"}
+
+        for index, post_text in enumerate(normalized_posts, start=1):
+            if not post_text:
+                return {"success": False, "error": f"Thread item {index} is empty"}
+            if len(post_text) > self.POST_CHAR_LIMIT:
+                return {
+                    "success": False,
+                    "error": f"Thread item {index} exceeds {self.POST_CHAR_LIMIT} character limit",
+                    "length": len(post_text),
+                    "index": index,
+                }
+
+        can_post, reason = self.can_post(trigger=trigger)
+        if not can_post:
+            return {"success": False, "error": reason}
+
+        results: List[Dict] = []
+        previous_tweet_id: Optional[str] = None
+
+        if not self.api_enabled:
+            for index, post_text in enumerate(normalized_posts, start=1):
+                results.append(
+                    {
+                        "success": True,
+                        "dry_run": True,
+                        "message": "API not configured - dry run mode",
+                        "tweet_id": f"dry_run_{index}",
+                        "text": post_text,
+                        "reply_to_tweet_id": previous_tweet_id,
+                    }
+                )
+                previous_tweet_id = f"dry_run_{index}"
+        else:
+            for post_text in normalized_posts:
+                try:
+                    result = self._send_tweet_with_retries(post_text, in_reply_to_tweet_id=previous_tweet_id)
+                except Exception as exc:
+                    return {"success": False, "error": str(exc), "results": results}
+                if not result.get("success"):
+                    result["results"] = results
+                    return result
+                results.append(result)
+                previous_tweet_id = result.get("tweet_id")
+
+        tweet_ids = [str(result.get("tweet_id")) for result in results if result.get("tweet_id")]
+        thread_urls = [f"https://x.com/i/web/status/{tweet_id}" for tweet_id in tweet_ids]
+
+        post_entry = {
+            "date": datetime.now().isoformat(),
+            "trigger": trigger,
+            "text": normalized_posts[0][:100] + "..." if len(normalized_posts[0]) > 100 else normalized_posts[0],
+            "success": True,
+            "dry_run": all(result.get("dry_run", False) for result in results),
+            "thread": True,
+            "thread_count": len(normalized_posts),
+            "tweet_ids": tweet_ids,
+        }
+        self.post_log["posts"].insert(0, post_entry)
+        self.post_log["posts"] = self.post_log["posts"][:100]
+        self.post_log["last_post_time"] = datetime.now().isoformat()
+        self._save_post_log()
+
+        return {
+            "success": True,
+            "message": "Thread posted successfully",
+            "tweet_ids": tweet_ids,
+            "thread_urls": thread_urls,
+            "results": results,
+        }
+
+    def _send_tweet(self, text: str, in_reply_to_tweet_id: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict:
+        """
+        Send a tweet using Twitter API v2.
+
+        Requires tweepy library: pip install tweepy
+        """
         try:
             import tweepy
         except ImportError:
@@ -450,16 +534,21 @@
                 access_token_secret=self._get_twitter_credential("twitter_access_secret", "TWITTER_ACCESS_SECRET")
             )
 
-            response = client.create_tweet(text=text)
-
-            return {
-                "success": True,
-                "tweet_id": response.data["id"],
-                "message": "Tweet posted successfully"
-            }
-
-        except tweepy.TweepyException as e:
-            return {"success": False, "error": str(e)}
+            create_kwargs = {"text": text}
+            if in_reply_to_tweet_id:
+                create_kwargs["in_reply_to_tweet_id"] = in_reply_to_tweet_id

+            return {
+                "success": True,
+                "tweet_id": response.data["id"],
+                "message": "Tweet posted successfully",
+                "reply_to_tweet_id": in_reply_to_tweet_id,
+            }
+
+        except tweepy.TweepyException as e:
+            return {"success": False, "error": str(e)}
 
     def check_and_post_triggers(self, current_metrics: Dict, previous_metrics: Optional[Dict] = None) -> List[Dict]:
         """
diff --git a/breakout/fs/tests/test_run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py b/breakout/fs/tests/test_run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..8c9d21e7c72639e7d69c7bfcb1438bed6df114b3
--- /dev/null
+++ b/breakout/fs/tests/test_run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py
@@ -0,0 +1,93 @@
+import json
+import shutil
+import sys
+import time
+import unittest
+from pathlib import Path
+from unittest.mock import patch
+
+
+FS_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
+if str(FS_DIR) not in sys.path:
+    sys.path.insert(0, str(FS_DIR))
+
+import run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow as workflow  # noqa: E402
+
+
+class RunTwitterTop5MultiProductWorkflowTests(unittest.TestCase):
+    def make_case_root(self, case_name: str) -> Path:
+        root = FS_DIR / "tests" / "_tmp" / case_name
+        shutil.rmtree(root, ignore_errors=True)
+        root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+        return root
+
+    def test_validate_payload_accepts_compact_thread_posts(self) -> None:
+        root = self.make_case_root("top5_validate_accepts")
+        package_root = root / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package" / "2026-04-04"
+        package_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+
+        package_payload = {
+            "packages": [
+                {"product_type": "forex"},
+                {"product_type": "indices"},
+                {"product_type": "metals"},
+                {"product_type": "energy"},
+            ],
+            "top5_multi_product_thread_posts": [
+                "The Strategy Warehouse multi-product top 5 | Update at 2026-04-04 01:00 | Single-contract basis. Product detail posts follow. #StrategyWarehouse #FuturesTrading #AlgoTrading",
+                "Forex | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. GBPNZD brk R 2 tp50 sl5 1370 | Single-contract basis. #Forex #SystemTrading",
+                "Indices | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. NQ brk 2 tp20 sl5 7505 | Single-contract basis. #Indices #SystemTrading",
+                "Metals | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. SI brk R 2 tp30 sl5 11360 | Single-contract basis. #Metals #SystemTrading",
+                "Energy | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. CL brk 2 tp20 sl5 6630 | Single-contract basis. #Energy #SystemTrading",
+            ],
+        }
+        package_path = package_root / "top5_weekly_posting_package.json"
+        package_path.write_text(json.dumps(package_payload), encoding="utf-8")
+        now = time.time()
+        Path(package_path).touch()
+
+        with patch.object(workflow, "PACKAGE_ROOT", root / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package"):
+            ok, details, posts = workflow._validate_payload("2026-04-04", now)
+
+        self.assertTrue(ok)
+        self.assertIn("Validated 5 thread posts", details)
+        self.assertEqual(len(posts), 5)
+
+    def test_validate_payload_rejects_multiline_product_post(self) -> None:
+        root = self.make_case_root("top5_validate_rejects_multiline")
+        package_root = root / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package" / "2026-04-04"
+        package_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+
+        package_payload = {
+            "packages": [
+                {"product_type": "forex"},
+                {"product_type": "indices"},
+                {"product_type": "metals"},
+                {"product_type": "energy"},
+            ],
+            "top5_multi_product_thread_posts": [
+                "Lead post",
+                "Forex | Mar 30-Apr 05 |\n1. GBPNZD 1370 | Single-contract basis. #Forex #SystemTrading",
+                "Indices | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. NQ 7505 | Single-contract basis. #Indices #SystemTrading",
+                "Metals | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. SI 11360 | Single-contract basis. #Metals #SystemTrading",
+                "Energy | Mar 30-Apr 05 | 1. CL 6630 | Single-contract basis. #Energy #SystemTrading",
+            ],
+        }
+        package_path = package_root / "top5_weekly_posting_package.json"
+        package_path.write_text(json.dumps(package_payload), encoding="utf-8")
+        Path(package_path).touch()
+
+        with patch.object(workflow, "PACKAGE_ROOT", root / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package"):
+            ok, details, posts = workflow._validate_payload("2026-04-04", time.time())
+
+        self.assertFalse(ok)
+        self.assertIn("single-line compact entry", details)
+        self.assertEqual(posts, [])
+
+    @classmethod
+    def tearDownClass(cls) -> None:
+        shutil.rmtree(FS_DIR / "tests" / "_tmp", ignore_errors=True)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()
diff --git a/breakout/fs/tests/test_social_publisher_thread.py b/breakout/fs/tests/test_social_publisher_thread.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..fc6a1df043dfb9ee0670a391f901f0ce6f9c7a0d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/breakout/fs/tests/test_social_publisher_thread.py
@@ -0,0 +1,70 @@
+import sys
+import unittest
+from pathlib import Path
+from unittest.mock import Mock
+
+
+FS_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
+if str(FS_DIR) not in sys.path:
+    sys.path.insert(0, str(FS_DIR))
+
+from social_publisher import SocialPublisher  # noqa: E402
+
+
+class SocialPublisherThreadTests(unittest.TestCase):
+    def make_publisher(self) -> SocialPublisher:
+        publisher = SocialPublisher.__new__(SocialPublisher)
+        publisher.POST_CHAR_LIMIT = 280
+        publisher.api_enabled = True
+        publisher.post_log = {"posts": [], "last_post_time": None}
+        publisher._save_post_log = Mock()
+        publisher.can_post = Mock(return_value=(True, "ok"))
+        publisher._send_tweet_with_retries = Mock(
+            side_effect=[
+                {"success": True, "tweet_id": "111"},
+                {"success": True, "tweet_id": "222"},
+                {"success": True, "tweet_id": "333"},
+            ]
+        )
+        return publisher
+
+    def test_publish_thread_posts_replies_in_sequence(self) -> None:
+        publisher = self.make_publisher()
+
+        result = publisher.publish_thread(
+            ["Lead post", "Reply one", "Reply two"],
+            trigger="breakout_top5_multi_product_every_6_hours",
+        )
+
+        self.assertTrue(result["success"])
+        self.assertEqual(result["tweet_ids"], ["111", "222", "333"])
+        self.assertEqual(
+            publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.call_args_list[0].args,
+            ("Lead post",),
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(
+            publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.call_args_list[0].kwargs,
+            {"in_reply_to_tweet_id": None},
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(
+            publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.call_args_list[1].kwargs,
+            {"in_reply_to_tweet_id": "111"},
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(
+            publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.call_args_list[2].kwargs,
+            {"in_reply_to_tweet_id": "222"},
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(publisher.post_log["posts"][0]["thread_count"], 3)
+
+    def test_publish_thread_rejects_long_post(self) -> None:
+        publisher = self.make_publisher()
+
+        result = publisher.publish_thread(["x" * 281], trigger="test")
+
+        self.assertFalse(result["success"])
+        self.assertIn("exceeds 280", result["error"])
+        publisher._send_tweet_with_retries.assert_not_called()
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()

+def build_top5_thread_posts(

+) -> List[str]:

+        f"{brand} multi-product top 5 | Update at {update_label} | "
+        f"Single-contract basis. Product detail posts follow. {' '.join(CONSOLIDATED_HASHTAGS)}"
+    )
+    posts = [intro_post]
+
+    for entry in package_entries:
+        product_type = title_case_product_type(str(entry["product_type"]))
+        date_label = format_date_range(entry.get("date_range", []))
+        top_strategies = entry.get("top_strategies", [])
+        ranking_text = " | ".join(
+            f"{item['rank']}. {item['product']} {item['strategy']} {format_post_points(item['total_net'])}"
+            for item in top_strategies
+        )
+        hashtags = f"#{product_type.replace(' ', '')} #SystemTrading"
+        post_text = (
+            f"{product_type} | {date_label} | {ranking_text} | "
+            f"Single-contract basis. {hashtags}"
+        )
+        posts.append(post_text)
+
+    return posts
+
+

diff --git a/breakout/fs/run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py b/breakout/fs/run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..71379cbccb446f43c9ad788c8db4c4d5cce6eaf4
--- /dev/null
+++ b/breakout/fs/run_twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow.py
@@ -0,0 +1,177 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import datetime
+import json
+import subprocess
+import sys
+import time
+from pathlib import Path
+
+from social_publisher import SocialPublisher
+
+
+ROOT = Path(r"C:\Users\edebe\eds\TradeApps\breakout\fs")
+GENERATE_SCRIPT = ROOT / "tools" / "social_posting_package" / "generate_posting_package.py"
+WORKFLOW_STATUS = ROOT / "twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow_status.json"
+WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT = ROOT / "twitter_top5_multi_product_workflow_result.json"
+PACKAGE_ROOT = ROOT / "json" / "live" / "social_posting_package"
+PACKAGE_FILENAME = "top5_weekly_posting_package.json"
+PACKAGE_PAYLOAD_KEY = "top5_multi_product_thread_posts"
+WORKFLOW_TRIGGER = "breakout_top5_multi_product_every_6_hours"
+
+
+def _write_status(status: dict) -> None:
+    WORKFLOW_STATUS.write_text(json.dumps(status, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
+
+
+def _new_status(run_date: str) -> dict:
+    return {
+        "timestamp": datetime.datetime.now().isoformat(),
+        "run_date": run_date,
+        "steps": {
+            "generate_content": {"ok": False, "details": "", "artifact": str(PACKAGE_ROOT / run_date / PACKAGE_FILENAME)},
+            "prepare_payload": {"ok": False, "details": "", "artifact": str(PACKAGE_ROOT / run_date / PACKAGE_FILENAME)},
+            "submit_post": {"ok": False, "details": "", "artifact": str(WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT)},
+            "record_outcome": {"ok": False, "details": "", "artifact": str(WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT)},
+        },
+        "final_status": "failed",
+    }
+
+
+def _mark_step(status: dict, step: str, ok: bool, details: str) -> None:
+    status["steps"][step]["ok"] = ok
+    status["steps"][step]["details"] = details
+    _write_status(status)
+
+
+def _run_command(command: list[str]) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
+    return subprocess.run(
+        command,
+        cwd=ROOT,
+        text=True,
+        encoding="utf-8",
+        errors="replace",
+        capture_output=True,
+        timeout=180000,
+    )
+
+
+def _load_package_json(run_date: str) -> dict:
+    package_path = PACKAGE_ROOT / run_date / PACKAGE_FILENAME
+    if not package_path.exists():
+        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Posting package not found: {package_path}")
+    return json.loads(package_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
+
+
+def _validate_payload(run_date: str, generated_started_at: float) -> tuple[bool, str, list[str]]:
+    package_path = PACKAGE_ROOT / run_date / PACKAGE_FILENAME
+    if not package_path.exists():
+        return False, f"Posting package was not created: {package_path}", []
+
+    if package_path.stat().st_mtime < generated_started_at - 1:
+        return False, f"Posting package was not refreshed by the current generator run: {package_path}", []
+
+    package = _load_package_json(run_date)
+    posts = package.get(PACKAGE_PAYLOAD_KEY)
+    if not isinstance(posts, list) or not posts:
+        return False, f"Posting package is missing {PACKAGE_PAYLOAD_KEY}", []
+
+    expected_count = 1 + len(package.get("packages", []))
+    if len(posts) != expected_count:
+        return False, f"Expected {expected_count} thread posts but found {len(posts)}", []
+
+    required_product_types = {"forex", "indices", "metals", "energy"}
+    seen_product_types: set[str] = set()
+    for index, post_text in enumerate(posts, start=1):
+        text = str(post_text or "").strip()
+        if not text:
+            return False, f"Thread post {index} is empty", []
+        if len(text) > 280:
+            return False, f"Thread post {index} exceeds 280 characters ({len(text)})", []
+        if index > 1:
+            if "\n" in text:
+                return False, f"Thread post {index} must remain a single-line compact entry", []
+            prefix = text.split("|", 1)[0].strip().lower()
+            seen_product_types.add(prefix)
+
+    missing = required_product_types - seen_product_types
+    if missing:
+        return False, f"Thread payload is missing product entries for: {sorted(missing)}", []
+
+    details = f"Validated {len(posts)} thread posts in {package_path}"
+    return True, details, [str(post).strip() for post in posts]
+
+
+def _write_result_artifact(run_date: str, thread_posts: list[str], publish_result: dict) -> None:
+    artifact = {
+        "timestamp": datetime.datetime.now().isoformat(),
+        "run_date": run_date,
+        "trigger": WORKFLOW_TRIGGER,
+        "thread_posts": [
+            {
+                "index": index,
+                "length": len(post_text),
+                "text": post_text,
+            }
+            for index, post_text in enumerate(thread_posts, start=1)
+        ],
+        "publish_result": publish_result,
+    }
+    WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT.write_text(json.dumps(artifact, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
+
+
+def main() -> int:
+    run_date = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else datetime.date.today().isoformat()
+    status = _new_status(run_date)
+    _write_status(status)
+
+    generate_started_at = time.time()
+    generate_result = _run_command(["python", str(GENERATE_SCRIPT), "--date", run_date])
+    if generate_result.returncode != 0:
+        _mark_step(
+            status,
+            "generate_content",
+            False,
+            f"Generator failed with code {generate_result.returncode}: {generate_result.stdout}\n{generate_result.stderr}",
+        )
+        return 1
+    _mark_step(status, "generate_content", True, generate_result.stdout.strip() or "Generator completed successfully")
+
+    try:
+        payload_ok, payload_details, thread_posts = _validate_payload(run_date, generate_started_at)
+    except Exception as exc:
+        _mark_step(status, "prepare_payload", False, f"Payload validation failed: {exc}")
+        return 1
+    if not payload_ok:
+        _mark_step(status, "prepare_payload", False, payload_details)
+        return 1
+    _mark_step(status, "prepare_payload", True, payload_details)
+
+    publisher = SocialPublisher()
+    publish_result = publisher.publish_thread(thread_posts, trigger=WORKFLOW_TRIGGER)
+    _write_result_artifact(run_date, thread_posts, publish_result)
+
+    if not publish_result.get("success"):
+        _mark_step(status, "submit_post", False, str(publish_result))
+        return 1
+    _mark_step(status, "submit_post", True, str(publish_result))
+
+    tweet_ids = publish_result.get("tweet_ids") or []
+    thread_urls = publish_result.get("thread_urls") or []
+    if not tweet_ids:
+        _mark_step(status, "record_outcome", False, f"No tweet IDs returned: {publish_result}")
+        return 1
+
+    _mark_step(
+        status,
+        "record_outcome",
+        True,
+        f"Recorded {len(tweet_ids)} tweet IDs and {len(thread_urls)} URLs in {WORKFLOW_ARTIFACT}",
+    )
+    status["final_status"] = "success"
+    _write_status(status)
+    return 0
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    raise SystemExit(main())

+        self._save_post_log()

+
+    def publish_thread(self, posts: List[str], trigger: str = "manual_thread") -> Dict:
+        """
+        Publish a reply thread where each post replies to the previous post.
+
+        Args:
+            posts: Ordered list of prepared post bodies.
+            trigger: Trigger name for rate-limit and audit logging.
+
+        Returns:
+            Result dict with thread publish details.
+        """
+        normalized_posts = [str(post or "").strip() for post in posts]
+        if not normalized_posts or not any(normalized_posts):
+            return {"success": False, "error": "At least one non-empty post is required"}
+
+        for index, post_text in enumerate(normalized_posts, start=1):
+            if not post_text:
+                return {"success": False, "error": f"Thread item {index} is empty"}
+            if len(post_text) > self.POST_CHAR_LIMIT:
+                return {
+                    "success": False,
+                    "error": f"Thread item {index} exceeds {self.POST_CHAR_LIMIT} character limit",
+                    "length": len(post_text),
+                    "index": index,
+                }
+
+        can_post, reason = self.can_post(trigger=trigger)
+        if not can_post:
+            return {"success": False, "error": reason}
+
+        results: List[Dict] = []
+        previous_tweet_id: Optional[str] = None
+
+        if not self.api_enabled:
+            for index, post_text in enumerate(normalized_posts, start=1):
+                results.append(
+                    {
+                        "success": True,
+                        "dry_run": True,
+                        "message": "API not configured - dry run mode",
+                        "tweet_id": f"dry_run_{index}",
+                        "text": post_text,
+                        "reply_to_tweet_id": previous_tweet_id,
+                    }
+                )
+                previous_tweet_id = f"dry_run_{index}"
+        else:
+            for post_text in normalized_posts:
+                try:
+                    result = self._send_tweet_with_retries(post_text, in_reply_to_tweet_id=previous_tweet_id)
+                except Exception as exc:
+                    return {"success": False, "error": str(exc), "results": results}
+                if not result.get("success"):
+                    result["results"] = results
+                    return result
+                results.append(result)
+                previous_tweet_id = result.get("tweet_id")
+
+        tweet_ids = [str(result.get("tweet_id")) for result in results if result.get("tweet_id")]
+        thread_urls = [f"https://x.com/i/web/status/{tweet_id}" for tweet_id in tweet_ids]
+
+        post_entry = {
+            "date": datetime.now().isoformat(),
+            "trigger": trigger,
+            "text": normalized_posts[0][:100] + "..." if len(normalized_posts[0]) > 100 else normalized_posts[0],
+            "success": True,
+            "dry_run": all(result.get("dry_run", False) for result in results),
+            "thread": True,
+            "thread_count": len(normalized_posts),
+            "tweet_ids": tweet_ids,
+        }
+        self.post_log["posts"].insert(0, post_entry)
+        self.post_log["posts"] = self.post_log["posts"][:100]
+        self.post_log["last_post_time"] = datetime.now().isoformat()
+        self._save_post_log()
+
+        return {
+            "success": True,
+            "message": "Thread posted successfully",
+            "tweet_ids": tweet_ids,
+            "thread_urls": thread_urls,
+            "results": results,
+        }
+
+    def _send_tweet(self, text: str, in_reply_to_tweet_id: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict:
+        """
+        Send a tweet using Twitter API v2.
+
+        Requires tweepy library: pip install tweepy
+        """
         try:
             import tweepy
         except ImportError:
@@ -450,16 +534,21 @@
                 access_token_secret=self._get_twitter_credential("twitter_access_secret", "TWITTER_ACCESS_SECRET")
             )

-
-            return {
-                "success": True,
-                "tweet_id": response.data["id"],
-                "message": "Tweet posted successfully"
-            }
-
-        except tweepy.TweepyException as e:
-            return {"success": False, "error": str(e)}
